pages.title: What Robinhood Chain actually is
pages.description: Architecture, chain IDs, and what's real vs hype — the no-token fact, the US-person restriction, and permissionless deployment, all sourced.
pages.whatYouBuild: A correct mental model of the chain: what it is, what it is *not*, and the two facts that trip up every newcomer.
prerequisites: Curiosity. No wallet or code yet.

pages.title: Connect and read the chain in 5 minutes
pages.description: Add the network to a wallet, get testnet funds, and make your first read against mainnet with viem — a real block number and gas price in your terminal.
pages.whatYouBuild: A tiny script that connects to Robinhood Chain and prints the live block height, chain ID, and gas price.

pages.title: Stock Tokens explained for developers
pages.description: ERC-20 + a Chainlink feed + ERC-8056 uiMultiplier(). The split/dividend trap, with a worked example showing the wrong and the right valuation.
pages.whatYouBuild: The one piece of domain knowledge that separates a correct Robinhood Chain app from a subtly broken one.

pages.title: Your first app: a live price ticker
pages.description: Build a terminal price ticker that streams multiplier-adjusted Chainlink quotes for any Stock Token, with staleness handling done right.
pages.whatYouBuild: A live-updating terminal ticker for AAPL, TSLA, NVDA and SPY — real prices, refreshing on an interval.

pages.title: Portfolio tracker done right
pages.description: Value any address's Stock Token holdings in one multicall — and understand exactly why a naive tracker reports the wrong number after a split.
pages.whatYouBuild: A portfolio valuation tool that is correct after corporate actions, cross-checked against the token's own on-chain share accounting.

pages.title: Swapping on-chain: quotes, slippage, execution
pages.description: Quote a Uniswap v3 swap across every fee tier, set slippage correctly, and execute on testnet — with a Stock Token eligibility sidebar.
pages.whatYouBuild: A working swap: a real mainnet quote you can run now, and the exact, safe path to a real testnet swap transaction.
prerequisites: A wallet private key in an env var for the write step (testnet)

pages.title: Streaming the chain: launchpads + firehose
pages.description: Watch NOXA and The Odyssey for new memecoin launches, then tap the raw sequencer firehose to see transactions ~100ms before they're queryable.
pages.whatYouBuild: Two live streams: a launch watcher printing new tokens as they deploy, and a firehose counter clocking hundreds of transactions per second.

pages.title: Sell your API for USDG with x402
pages.description: Put a Stock Token data endpoint behind an HTTP 402 paywall settled in USDG, and take your first real on-chain payment — from zero to a paid request.
pages.whatYouBuild: A paid API: an endpoint that returns 402 Payment Required, verifies a USDG payment on-chain, and then serves the data.

pages.title: Give your AI agent chain access with MCP
pages.description: Build a Model Context Protocol server that exposes Robinhood Chain reads as tools, then wire it into Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or Cursor — with a real transcript.
pages.whatYouBuild: An MCP server your AI assistant can call to quote Stock Tokens and read portfolios live, plus the exact config to connect it.
prerequisites: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or Cursor installed

pages.title: Build an autonomous trading agent
pages.description: A paper-mode trading agent with a real strategy, hard spend caps, and a kill switch — risk-first framing before a single lira of real capital.
pages.whatYouBuild: An agent that loops over live quotes, evaluates a strategy, and would trade — running safely in paper mode with real market data.
prerequisites: You have read the risk section. Twice.

pages.title: Launch a coin programmatically
pages.description: How Robinhood Chain's launchpads work, how to decode their events, and the responsible path to launching a token on testnet from code.
pages.whatYouBuild: A launch monitor that decodes real NOXA and Odyssey events, and a clear, responsibly-framed recipe for launching your own test token.
prerequisites: A funded testnet wallet for the write step

pages.title: Ship it: deploy and go mainnet
pages.description: Deploy your static app to Pages, your API to Cloud Run, and walk the going-mainnet checklist: funding, keys, spend caps, and monitoring.
pages.whatYouBuild: A deployment plan and a mainnet-readiness checklist you can actually tick off before you flip the switch.
prerequisites: A deploy target (GitHub Pages and/or Google Cloud Run)
